name: San Juan and Los Tientos Circular Trail
name_original: Subida a San Juan y Los Tientos —  circular por el este del Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile Ascent to San Juan and Los Tientos circular route in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, with views and forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Peguerinos offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, bordering Madrid and Segovia. The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, numerous reservoirs, and accessible mountain peaks. This area provides a variety of trails, from gentle walks around water bodies to more challenging ascents into the mountains. Best hiking trails around Peguerinos The most popular hiking route is San Juan and Los Tientos Circular Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route traverses the eastern part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cueva Valiente Loop from San Rafael, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This trail leads to Cueva Valiente mountain, offering a rewarding challenge with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Cabeza Líjar and Mirador Peña del Aguila — circular route through the west of the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through the western side of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Peguerinos is defined by pine forests, clear reservoirs, and mountain peaks within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy reservoir loops to moderate mountain ascents. The routes in Peguerinos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Peguerinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Peguerinos?
A: Peguerinos, nestled within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find everything from gentle walks around picturesque reservoirs and through extensive pine forests to more challenging ascents to mountain peaks. The area caters to various skill levels, with 3 easy, 29 moderate, and 2 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Peguerinos?
A: Yes, Peguerinos has several easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed stroll. The region's reservoirs, like La Aceña and Cañada Mojada, offer tranquil paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356943573/canada-mojada-reservoir-and-llanillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Mojada Reservoir and Llanillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers gentler terrain around the water, suitable for a pleasant outing.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Peguerinos?
A: The Peguerinos area is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore extensive pine forests, several reservoirs like La Aceña and Cañada Mojada, and even the unique granite formation of Peña de Cenicientos. Hikers can also discover mountain peaks such as Cueva Valiente and Cabeza Líjar. For specific points of interest, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2505177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6556720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/355629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Líjar Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Peguerinos?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Peguerinos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356943847/san-juan-and-los-tientos-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan and Los Tientos Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses the eastern Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145197648/ascent-to-cabeza-lijar-and-mirador-pena-del-aguila-circular-route-through-the-west-of-the-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cabeza Líjar and Mirador Peña del Aguila&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the western side of the park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Peguerinos?
A: The hiking trails in Peguerinos are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from serene reservoir loops to rewarding mountain ascents, and the well-maintained paths within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Peguerinos?
A: Peguerinos offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming wildflowers or colorful foliage. Summer is ideal for shaded forest paths, while winter can offer snowy landscapes for those prepared for colder conditions and potential snowshoeing, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Peguerinos?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Peguerinos offers routes with significant elevation gain and rewarding views. The ascent to peaks like Cueva Valiente (1,903 m) or Cabeza Líjar (1,823 m) provides a demanding trek. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145278208/cueva-valiente-loop-from-san-rafael" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Valiente Loop from San Rafael&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that includes a substantial climb.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Peguerinos?
A: Peguerinos is a tranquil mountain village, and while specific cafes or pubs might be limited directly on the trails, the village itself offers options for refreshments and meals. It's always a good idea to check local establishments in Peguerinos for their opening hours before or after your hike.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Peguerinos has a rich history that can be explored on its trails. Some routes lead to the remains of the Wolfram Mine, which played a role during World War II. Additionally, certain trails traverse trenches from the Civil War, offering a poignant glimpse into the past. The area also features several shelters, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2636724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;, which have their own stories.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Peguerinos area?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available in the Peguerinos area, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences. This includes 3 easy, 29 moderate, and 2 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the trails around Peguerinos?
A: Hiking in Peguerinos offers breathtaking panoramic views, especially from higher elevations. You can expect sweeping vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, extensive pine forests, and the surrounding valleys. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145197648/ascent-to-cabeza-lijar-and-mirador-pena-del-aguila-circular-route-through-the-west-of-the-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cabeza Líjar and Mirador Peña del Aguila&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent viewpoints, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2806003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Valley of the Fallen Cross from Refugio de la Naranjera&lt;/a&gt; is particularly notable.

Q: Are there any trails that feature reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, the Peguerinos region is known for its picturesque reservoirs and lagoons. The La Aceña Reservoir is excellent for bird watching and tranquil walks, while the Cañada Mojada Reservoir offers opportunities for fishing and picnics. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356944191/tobar-and-acena-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobar and Aceña Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to explore these water features.
tips: Once you reach the Naranjera refuge, just beyond the fence, there are some rocks from where there are magnificent views of the entire valley of the fallen. You have to climb carefully, but it is accessible. Not suitable for young children
One of the classic peaks of the Guadarrama mountain range. Easy ascent along the GR-10 and good views from the summit.

It is an essential point of passage in the hiking and mountaineering routes in the Abantos peak because it has fresh water all year round. The detour to the spring is not indicated, it is a path that leaves from the right slope of the mountain track near the crossroads where the path descends from the Abantos summit. In the meadow of the fountain there are marks of the long-distance trail GR-10, which passes through the water pipe and descends to San Lorenzo de El Escorial through the pine forests of the mountain range.

faqs.question: How long does the Ascent to San Juan and Los Tientos hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike is approximately 10.4 km long and generally takes around 3 hours and 16 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, stops for photos, and fitness level.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this trail involves an elevation gain of about 247 meters. While not overly strenuous, it's best suited for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. Beginners who are accustomed to some physical activity should find it manageable, but be prepared for rocky and uneven sections.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to San Juan and Los Tientos circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The specific starting point for this Komoot route would be detailed on the tour page. Given its location in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, parking is typically available at designated trailheads or nearby villages like San Lorenzo de El Escorial. It's advisable to check the Komoot tour map for precise trailhead coordinates and parking information before you go.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain characteristic of the Sierra de Guadarrama. This includes rocky and uneven paths, especially at higher altitudes, with sections potentially passing through pine forests, oak groves, and meadows. Some parts might be stony and could be slippery when wet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include a View of the Valley of the Fallen, the Mount Abantos summit, and the Cervunal Spring. These offer scenic beauty and opportunities for rest and appreciation of the surroundings.

faqs.question: What wildlife or flora might I encounter in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park is rich in biodiversity. You might spot Iberian ibex, deer, wild boar, and various bird species such as the Spanish imperial eagle and Eurasian black vulture. The flora includes extensive pine forests, oak groves, and diverse mountain vegetation.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in the Sierra de Guadarrama typically depends on your preferences. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended. In winter, higher sections may have snow, potentially requiring specialized equipment like crampons, so check conditions beforehand.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park dog-friendly, and what are the rules for pets on this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in many areas of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check the specific regulations for the national park or any protected areas you plan to enter, as rules can vary.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no general entrance fees to access the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park for hiking. However, certain activities or specific areas might require permits, especially for large groups or during peak seasons. It's always a good idea to check the official park website for the most current regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable trails in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde el Puerto de la Fuenfría a San Lorenzo del Escorial), Ruta Verde Guadarrama - El Escorial, and various segments of the GR 10. It also briefly touches upon the Camino de Abantos and La Lápida.

faqs.question: What should I know about weather conditions and what to wear for this hike?
faqs.answer: Weather in the mountains can change rapidly. Even on a sunny day, temperatures can drop, especially at higher elevations. It's crucial to wear layers, including a waterproof and windproof outer shell. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential due to the rocky and uneven terrain. Always check the local forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park is close to several charming villages, such as San Lorenzo de El Escorial. These towns typically offer a range of amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. You can find places to eat or stay before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: View of the Valley of the Fallen
